MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lindsay Weiss of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Marian Evans, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Charles Evans, Jr., deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Charles Evans, Jr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant did not demonstrate that no person has knowledge that Plant Maintenance employees worked in the presence of decedent, which defendant confirmed was the sole issue raised by its motion. The deposition testimony of Monte Manwill and Robert Mingram preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Additionally, defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections nos. 1 through 4 are sustained and objection no. 5 is overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-02-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-02-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Marian Evans, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Charles Evans, Jr., deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ATKN COMPANY (FKA GUY F. ATKINSON COMPANY)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Atkn Company's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' Negligence II (Survival) and Negligence II (Wrongful Death) causes of action are denied. On the issue of duty, defendant's motion fails for the following reasons, any of which are sufficient on their own to warrant denial: Even if defendant could successfully invoke the sophisticated user defense, that defense does not completely dispose of the issue of duty or of plaintiffs' two causes of action. The defense applies only to failure to warn claims and defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs' claims are based solely on a failure to warn theory. (See Johnson v. American Standard, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal.4th 56; Johnson v. Honeywell International Inc. (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 549, 559; Webb v. Special Electric Co., Inc. (2016) 63 Cal.4th 167, 187.) Defendant did not properly plead the sophisticated user defense as it pertains to decedent Charles Evans, Jr. in its answer. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Defendant's evidence does not demonstrate that decedent knew, or should have known, of the hazards of asbestos-containing insulation during the time he worked at the Willamette Shipyard in the late-1960s. (Johnson v. American Standard, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal.4th 56.) On the issue of causation, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Decedent's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826 or, alternatively, it is sufficient to create a triable issue whether the laborers at issue were employed by Willamette Shipyard, the sole issue raised by defendant's causation arguments. DiCola v. White Bros. Performance Products, Inc. (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 666 does not bar decedent's identification of the name on the hard hats. Plaintiffs' objections nos. 1 through 5 are sustained. The Court did not consider defendant's reply papers for failure to lodge a courtesy copy. (S.F. Local Rules 2.7(B), 2.11(T).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)

Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Charles Evans and Marian Evans
John Rosenthal of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant /opposing party Golden Gate Drywall
(Refer to Sign-in sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. Plaintiffs informed the Court that defendants Lorentzen Co., Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc. and Pacific Ship Repair 1960, Inc. were dismissed on Jan. 25, 2016. After discussion off the record, on the record the court adopted its tentative ruling as revised: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is May 23, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is May 25, 2016. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is May 12, 2016. Plaintiff stipulates that Summary judgment/adjudication motions can be brought on 50 days notice, with plaintiffs' opposition due 10 days before hearing and replies due 5 days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 18, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than April 1, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by May 16, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on May 19, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 6, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is May 20, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 16, 2016. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than February 4, 2016 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. For all persons with knowledge who are identified as being represented by Brayton Purcell and whose depositions are noticed pursuant to subpoena by defendant(s), the depositions must be completed by March 16, 2016. No later than February 4, 2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry.)
